steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The function is $K(v) = \frac{5}{2}v^2$, where $v$ is speed in meters per second and $K(v)$ is kinetic energy in joules. The notation $K(18) = 810$ means the input to the function is $v = 18$ and the output is $K = 810$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: The input $v = 18$ represents a speed of 18 meters per second, and the output $K = 810$ represents a kinetic energy of 810 joules.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
• Choice A: The object traveling at 18 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 810 joules. — This matches $v = 18$ and $K = 810$ exactly. That matches Choice A — The object traveling at 18 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 810 joules..
  • Choice B: The object traveling at 180 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 810 joules. — Eliminated.
  • Choice C: The object traveling at 810 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 18 joules. — Eliminated.
  • Choice D: The object traveling at 2,025 meters per second has a kinetic energy of 18 joules. — Eliminated.
